# @zakkster/lite-headless/datepicker

createDatePicker(options) -> DatePickerHandle

Headless date-picker primitive. Second form-control in the package after
slider; NOT an overlay (no portal, no positioner, no dismiss). For the
common "open picker on input click" pattern, compose with createPopover:
the picker lives inside a popover's content tree, and the popover's
outside-click handling covers the cells via composedPath.

## Value shape

Value is always an array (matches slider precedent):

- single mode:  [Date | null]
- range mode:   [Date | null, Date | null]  -- sorted: start <= end when both set

While picking a range:
- before any click:    [null, null]
- after first click:   [startDate, null]
- after second click:  [startDate, endDate] (auto-sorted)
- third click resets:  [newStartDate, null]

All Date values are stripped to startOfDay (no time-of-day).

## Options

- mode: "single" | "range" = "single"
- value: WriteSignal<Date[]>           -- external controlled mode
- defaultValue: Date | Date[]
- onValueChange: (next, reason) => void
                                          reason: "pick"|"pick-start"|"pick-end"

- minDate: Date                        -- cells before get aria-disabled
- maxDate: Date                        -- cells after get aria-disabled
- weekStartsOn: 0..6 = 0               -- 0=Sunday, 1=Monday, etc.
- disabled: boolean = false

- now: () => Date = () => new Date()   -- override for testability
- today: Date | () => Date             -- if a function, the picker reacts to
                                          its return inside an effect.
                                          Drop-in for @zakkster/lite-time's
                                          midnight-rollover signal.

## Returned

{
    value(): Date[]                     -- snapshot, with fresh Date copies
    setValue(next, reason?),
    viewMonth: ReadSignal<Date>         -- the displayed month (day=1)
    focusedDate: ReadSignal<Date>       -- the keyboard cursor's date
    hoverDate: ReadSignal<Date|null>    -- range-preview pointer location
    view: ReadSignal<"days"|"months"|"years">   -- v0.7
    setView(v), cycleView(),                   -- v0.7
    goToPrevMonth(), goToNextMonth(),
    goToMonth(date),
    getDaysInView(monthDate?): Date[]   -- 42 dates for the grid view
    getMonthsInView(year?): Date[]      -- 12 months (v0.7)
    getYearsInView(year?): Date[]       -- 12 years incl. 2 padding (v0.7)
    weekStartsOn, mode, minDate, maxDate,

    attachGrid(el),                     -- gets role="grid"; data-view mirrors signal
    attachGridContainer(el),            -- listens for pointerleave to clear preview
    attachDay(el, date),                -- per-cell; idempotent on same el
    attachMonth(el, monthDate),         -- v0.7: months-view cell
    attachYear(el, yearDate),           -- v0.7: years-view cell
    attachPrevMonth(el),                -- click strides by view's unit
    attachNextMonth(el),                -- (month / year / decade)
    attachMonthLabel(el, opts?),        -- opts: { formatter, clickToCycle }

    destroy(), destroyed
}

## Grid rendering pattern

The consumer owns the markup; the picker attaches behavior:

```js
const picker = createDatePicker({ today: TODAY });
picker.attachGrid(gridEl);
picker.attachMonthLabel(monthEl);
picker.attachPrevMonth(prevBtn);
picker.attachNextMonth(nextBtn);

function render() {
    grid.innerHTML = "";
    for (const d of picker.getDaysInView()) {
        const cell = document.createElement("button");
        cell.textContent = String(d.getDate());
        grid.appendChild(cell);
        picker.attachDay(cell, d);
    }
}
render();
picker.viewMonth.subscribe(render);
```

Or reuse cells across months (no DOM churn):

```js
const cells = Array.from({ length: 42 }, () => {
    const b = document.createElement("button");
    grid.appendChild(b);
    return b;
});
function repaint() {
    const days = picker.getDaysInView();
    for (let i = 0; i < 42; i++) {
        cells[i].textContent = String(days[i].getDate());
        picker.attachDay(cells[i], days[i]);   // idempotent
    }
}
repaint();
picker.viewMonth.subscribe(repaint);
```

## Keyboard model (on the grid element)

  ArrowLeft / ArrowRight              -1 / +1 day
  ArrowUp / ArrowDown                 -7 / +7 days (prev/next week)
  PageUp / PageDown                   -1 / +1 month
  Shift+PageUp / Shift+PageDown       -1 / +1 year
  Home                                start of week (respects weekStartsOn)
  End                                 end of week
  Enter / Space                       pick the focused date

Crossing a month boundary auto-switches viewMonth.

## States exposed on cells

- role="gridcell"
- data-date="YYYY-M-D"                 stable key for the cell's date
- tabindex                            "0" on focused cell, "-1" on others (roving)
- data-focused                        present on focused cell
- data-today + aria-current="date"    today's cell
- data-selected + aria-selected       selected dates
- data-in-range                       (range mode) cells between start and end
- data-in-range-preview               (range mode, partial selection) hover preview
- data-range-start, data-range-end    (range mode) endpoints
- data-outside-month                  padding days from prev/next month
- data-disabled + aria-disabled       outside minDate/maxDate

## Integration: lite-time

For pickers left open across midnight (e.g. a sidebar permanent calendar),
pass a `today` function that subscribes to lite-time's day-rollover signal.
The picker's reactive effect will re-paint the today marker when midnight
hits. For ephemeral popover-wrapped pickers, omit `today` -- it's computed
once from `nowFn()` and that's fine.

## Integration: lite-form

The picker exposes a writable `value` signal that any form library can
consume as a field. Pass `value: form.field("departureDate")` (or the
equivalent) and the form library owns the value; the picker just renders
and emits onValueChange.

## Integration: createPopover

Wrap the picker's calendar markup inside a popover content element. The
popover's outside-click handler uses composedPath and treats the cells as
inside the content tree automatically. On selection (e.g. onValueChange
for single mode, or onValueChange with reason="pick-end" for range),
call popover.setOpen(false) to close.

## v0.7: year + decade views

The picker has a three-state view machine driven by the `view` signal:

- `"days"` (default) — 42-cell month grid (the v0.6 surface)
- `"months"` — 12-cell grid (Jan..Dec of the current viewMonth's year)
- `"years"` — 12-cell grid for the decade containing viewMonth's year
                (1 padding year before + 10 in-decade + 1 padding after)

Cycle days → months → years via `picker.cycleView()` or by wiring it to
the month label click:

    picker.attachMonthLabel(labelEl, { clickToCycle: true });

Render the months / years grids using the parallel helpers:

    picker.getMonthsInView()   // 12 Date objects, day=1 of each month
    picker.getYearsInView()    // 12 Date objects, Jan 1 of each year

Attach cells just like days. attachMonth / attachYear are idempotent
on the same element so the SAME 12 cells can be reused across
drilldown transitions:

    // first drilldown: render months
    picker.setView("months");
    for (let i = 0; i < 12; i++) picker.attachMonth(cells[i], months[i]);

    // later drilldown to years: replace bindings on the same cells
    picker.setView("years");
    for (let i = 0; i < 12; i++) picker.attachYear(cells[i], years[i]);

Per-view keyboard nav (on the grid):

- months view: ← → ±1 month, ↑ ↓ ±3 months (3-col layout),
               Page ±1 year, Home = Jan, End = Dec,
               Enter = drill to days view at focused month
- years view:  ← → ±1 year, ↑ ↓ ±3 years,
               Page ±10 years (decade), Home/End = decade boundaries,
               Enter = drill to months view at focused year

Per-view prev/next stride: month / year / decade. Per-view label format:
"June 2026" / "2026" / "2020 – 2029". Custom formatter receives
(viewMonth, view) so consumers can override per view if needed.

## Not in v0.7 (planned later)

- multi-date mode
- time-of-day picker (this is date-only)
- timezone-correct date math beyond what Date gives

## CSS contract

This primitive's painted attributes follow the canonical CSS contract
in `docs/CSS_CONTRACT.md`. See that doc for the cross-primitive
taxonomy (booleans like `data-open` / `data-disabled` / `data-loading`,
enums like `data-status` / `data-side`, ARIA pairings, host accessor
naming, and event naming).
